content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need outlines/prompts.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: since your feature adds the foundational create_jinja_env(filters=...) helper (a refactor of _template_from_str/_template_from_file), you go FIRST. I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read the file and layer my global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter + a module-level dict) additively on top of your helper — I'll have create_jinja_env merge in registry filters first, then apply local filters on top so local still wins. I won't touch prompt()/from_str/from_file structure, just the filter-merging point. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll wait for your DONE before editing.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Confirmed compatible. I will NOT rename _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt. I'll add: a module-level _FILTERS dict registry, register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) functions, and I'll update env.filters in _template_from_str/_template_from_file (the same spot you're refactoring into create_jinja_env) to merge in the global registry before any locally-passed filters are applied, so local filters win. Same function names as I described (register_filter/unregister_filter) — no collision with your create_jinja_env/filters param naming. Proceeding to implement now in my own sandbox.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Added module-level _FILTERS dict registry, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name), and env.filters.update(_FILTERS) in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (placed before the built-in name/description/source/signature/schema/args filters, so built-ins can't be shadowed, and before any local filters your code would apply, so local wins). No renames to existing functions/classes. Tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13 passed). Patch written to patch.txt in my sandbox.
